releases.title: TSR Reliability and Records Improvements
releases.summary: Create TSR became safer and easier to complete, while frequently used records pages gained better sorting.

items.description: Engineer signatures are now required before a TSR can be saved.
Client email suggestions are now collapsed to reduce confusion, and internal Shimadzu addresses are excluded from client suggestions.
A soft reminder now identifies important TSR details that are still blank before final saving.
Save and error notifications automatically scroll into view.
Starting a new TSR or opening a new session now clears stale form details.
Saved TSR drafts are now visible and can be reopened from a dedicated draft list.
The Actions Taken section now uses more of the available first-page space before continuing to another page.
Product columns can now be sorted, and the selected sort order is remembered.
Long Travel Request schedule cards now retain a readable and consistent width.

releases.title: TSR Preview, Draft, and Workflow Updates
releases.summary: Final TSR review became more dependable, with stronger drafts, better PDF handling, and cleaner operational records.

items.description: Multiline Remarks and Recommendations now continue correctly instead of being limited to one printed line.
The Department field is editable while Radiology remains the default value.
Medical Center columns can now be sorted for faster record lookup.
Personnel with approver-only access are no longer shown as field engineers on the Calendar.
Zero-amount schedule rows are excluded during final submission while remaining available in drafts.
Activity Logs now classify newer TSR, reimbursement, travel, cash advance, approval, and accounting events more clearly.
Engineers must now review the final TSR PDF before confirming Save TSR.
The final TSR preview was resized for a professional layout with a default 100 percent zoom.
Draft loading now filters out unrelated and empty ghost drafts while preserving legitimate engineer drafts.
Engineers may explicitly apply their saved Settings signature instead of drawing it for every TSR.

releases.title: TSR Email and Save Protection
releases.summary: Multiple TSR attachments are handled more reliably, and blank or incomplete TSR files are prevented.

items.description: Schedules with multiple generated or uploaded TSRs now include the complete attachment list when sending email.
Engineers can review which TSR files will be attached before sending an email.
Additional payload safeguards prevent completed TSR PDFs from being saved with blank engineer-entered details.
Missing equipment and serial information entered in Create TSR is now captured more reliably.

releases.title: Cleaner Reimbursement Reports
releases.summary: Reimbursement Excel files now keep system work context separate from user-entered remarks.

items.description: Generated Excel reports now use separate Work Details and Remarks columns.
Remarks remain blank by default instead of repeating the client, task, product, and equipment details.
Schedules and manual items without clients now use cleaner Work Details wording.

releases.title: Sharper TSR PDFs and Smarter Travel Requests
releases.summary: TSR output quality, Travel Request conflict handling, supporting documents, and system update communication received major improvements.

items.description: Newly generated TSR PDFs now use sharp vector text, lines, and form elements for clearer viewing and zooming.
Urgent or retroactive Travel Requests can proceed when an exact matching client schedule exists within the travel dates.
Non-client commitments and overlapping Travel Requests remain protected as real conflicts.
Travel Requests now support up to 10 optional PDF or image attachments, with files up to 35MB optimized to 2MB when possible.
Approvers can preview Travel Request supporting documents, and Accounting receives one compiled supporting-attachments PDF.
A new What's New page provides readable daily system updates with account-based unread badges and acknowledgements.
The What's New page now lets users acknowledge all of their unread updates at once.
The What's New sidebar label and unread badge now display clearly together on desktop and mobile.
What's New is now available from a compact bell button in the desktop and mobile headers instead of occupying a full sidebar row.

releases.title: Private Bucket Storage Cutover
releases.summary: Managed system uploads now use private Railway bucket storage with verified migration and protected access.

items.description: TSRs, schedule files, reimbursement receipts, Travel Request attachments, and liquidation receipts now use private bucket storage.
All migrated upload files passed size and SHA-256 verification before the production storage cutover.
Settings now displays private bucket usage, total capacity, remaining space, percentage, connection, verification, region, and active storage mode.
Travel Request details and attachments are now limited to the requester, listed participants, and the specifically assigned approver through their dedicated workflow.
Queued TSRs now recover automatically from temporary connection failures, avoid duplicate saves, and resume pending supporting-file uploads safely.

releases.title: Accounting Attachment Controls
releases.summary: Supporting documents and receipts are easier to manage safely across every accounting workflow.

items.description: Editable reimbursements, travel requests, and liquidations now provide clear individual Delete and Delete All controls for uploaded files.
Cash Advance requests now support optional PDF and image attachments that approvers can review and Accounting receives with the approved form.
Attachment deletion remains available for Draft, Rejected, or Returned records and is locked after submission or approval.
